daily_summary.headline: Exciting Aurora and Routine Launches Await

top_events.title: Aurora Viewing Opportunity
top_events.description: High geomagnetic activity expected on March 19, with Kp index reaching 6.33. Best viewing from 03:00 to 21:00 UTC, visible down to 55° latitude.
top_events.action: Plan for aurora viewing if skies are clear.

top_events.title: Falcon 9 Block 5 | Starlink Group 10-46
top_events.description: Routine launch of 29 Starlink satellites by SpaceX. Scheduled for March 17, 13:27 UTC from Cape Canaveral.
top_events.action: Watch live stream for launch coverage.

top_events.title: Zhuque-2E | Unknown Payload
top_events.description: Launch by LandSpace with an unknown payload, scheduled for March 19. Details are limited.
top_events.action: Stay tuned for updates on this mysterious mission.

alerts.title: High Geomagnetic Activity Alert
alerts.message: A strong geomagnetic storm is expected on March 19, 2026, with Kp index reaching 6.33. This provides a great opportunity for aurora viewing, visible down to 55° latitude.
alerts.action_required: Prepare for aurora viewing if you are in a suitable location with clear skies.

opportunities.recommendation: Good viewing opportunity for high-latitude observers. Worth checking if you're above 60° latitude and skies are clear.
Possible aurora at high latitudes. Best for northern locations (65°+). Worth checking if conditions are optimal.
